title: practical english usage - Part 245
content:
If we  use other structures (for example imperatives, should, had better), we  are 2 other     structures:       telling     people     to  do   things I wonder if you could (possibly) help me for a few minutes.

Indirect yes/no questions are also used in polite requests.

You couldn't lend me some money, could you? (informal) Would you like to help me for a few minutes?

Would you mind switching on the TV?

Could you possibly tell me the way to the station? (very polite) Some other typical structures used in requests: the time.) Could you tell me the time, please? (much more polite than Please tell me (This suggests that the hearer can choose whether to agree or not.) We   usually ask people to do things for us by means of yes/no questions.
